name: Di Ciccio
image_alt: Small blue-green broccoli heads with side shoots
description: A classic Italian sprouting broccoli that produces a small central head followed by an abundance of tender side shoots. Harvested over many weeks, it offers a longer season than heading varieties.
history: An heirloom from the Ciccio region of Italy, brought to America by Italian immigrants in the late 19th century. Favored by Italian-American home gardeners for generations for its extended harvest and rich flavor.
seasonal_quirks: Best planted for fall harvest in most climates. After the central head is cut, side shoots proliferate — production can continue for 4–6 weeks. Flavor peaks after light frost.
harvest_cues: Cut central head before any yellow flowers open — buds should be tight and deep green. Harvest side shoots at 3–5 inches, also before flowering.
variety_notes: The side-shoot production habit makes this the most practical broccoli for small gardens — one plant provides weeks of harvest rather than a single cut.
preservation: Blanch and freeze florets. Pickle florets in vinegar brine.
